name: Southern Chicken and Dumplings
recipeInstructions:
1. Tie the parsley and dill sprigs with thread. Cut the chicken into serving-sized pieces. Place the chicken and its giblets (discard the liver) in a large heavy pot. Add the carrots, onions, parsnips (if using), black pepper, chopped parsley or dill, and the tied sprigs. Add enough water to cover all ingredients. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 45-60 minutes or until the chicken is tender. Skim off any fat or foam as it rises to the surface.
2. When the chicken is done, remove and discard the parsley and dill sprigs. Using a slotted spoon, place the chicken and vegetables into a bowl and keep warm in an oven that has been slightly heated (then turned off).
3. For the dumplings: In a large bowl, combine flour with 1/2 teaspoon poultry seasoning or sage, 1/3 cup chopped parsley, 1/2 teaspoon black pepper, 1 large beaten egg, 1/2 cup milk, and 1/4 teaspoon salt (optional). Refrigerate the batter until ready to use.
4. Bring the broth to a boil and lower heat so it remains at a slow boil. Drop rounded teaspoons of batter into the broth. Cover tightly and simmer for 15 minutes. Do not remove the lid during this time.
5. Remove dumplings with a slotted spoon and add them to the chicken and vegetables. Spoon some of the broth over all and garnish with chopped parsley if desired. Serve immediately, placing the broth in cups for each serving.
